message:
Support -dev versions in OCSF documentation sync

Update the sync script to include development versions alongside
stable releases when generating OCSF documentation. Dev versions
now sort after their corresponding stable versions (e.g., 1.8.0
before 1.8.0-dev). Simplify the landing page wording to avoid
duplication between the version table introduction and other
sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,9 @@ our [release page](https://github.com/tenzir/docs/releases/tag/latest).
 
 ## Versions
 
+We publish all OCSF schema versions with full cross-references between classes,
+objects, profiles, and types.
+
 | Version                        | Classes | Objects | Profiles | Types |
 | ------------------------------ | ------- | ------- | -------- | ----- |
 | [1.7.0](/reference/ocsf/1-7-0) | 83      | 170     | 12       | 24    |
@@ -59,15 +62,9 @@ our [release page](https://github.com/tenzir/docs/releases/tag/latest).
 | [1.1.0](/reference/ocsf/1-1-0) | 50      | 106     | 8        | 22    |
 | [1.0.0](/reference/ocsf/1-0-0) | 36      | 84      | 6        | 22    |
 
-:::tip[Why embed the OCSF docs?]
-We publish this OCSF reference within the Tenzir documentation because our format
-is optimized for AI consumption. Every page is available as raw Markdown by
-appending `.md` to any URL, and all schema elements are fully cross-referenced
-with navigable links. This makes it easy for language models to understand and
-traverse the OCSF schema programmatically in a compact representation. Download
-an AI-optimized archive at our [docs release
+For complete offline processing, download an AI-optimized snapshot of our entire
+documentation at our [GitHub release
 page](https://github.com/tenzir/docs/releases/tag/latest).
-:::
 
 ## Using OCSF with Tenzir
